Hey everyone Sam Ovens here and welcome to this training module called Achieving Omni Presence. So in this module I'm going to show you really how you can plug the holes of your marketing campaign and your funnel and get more people to do what you want them to do. The way we do that is we achieve omnipresence and in this video I'm going to show you exactly how we do that. So here's what we're going to cover on today's training. So we're going to start off by discussing every marketer's nightmare and why people don't do what we want them to do. So we've got a funnel and we've got a process which we try to step people through. However, things don't always go as planned and most people actually don't take the path that we want them to take. And I'm going to show you how that happens, where the leakages are and what we can do about it. Then we're going to discuss achieving omnipresence and how to be everywhere and nowhere at the same time. So omnipresence is what we call retargeting on multiple platforms. So I'm sure you've experienced retargeting before. It's when a company, when you visit a company's website, you might view a product or something on Amazon. And then you leave the website and you don't buy it. And then for weeks you just keep seeing the ad for that exact product that you're about to buy everywhere on the internet. It's like the company is following you around. And that's what we call omnipresence. And I'm going to show you how we can do that and how we can literally be everywhere in your niche's world. And then I'm going to show you blueprints for setting up a flawless retargeting campaign. And I'm going to show you step by step on my screen as I go and set all of this up. So we're going to cover what the issue is and then we're going to cover how to solve it. We're going to discuss the high level strategy and then we're going to get right down into the step by step action steps. So let's get started. Every market is nightmare and why people don't do what we want them to do. So when it comes to marketing most people don't do what we want them to do. And we design an ideal path for people to follow but most people don't follow it the way we'd like them to. And let me show you exactly what I mean by this. So here's our paid client attraction method or our fragmentation funnel. And it starts off with Facebook ads then goes to the landing page then the value video. Then they schedule with schedule once in our calendar. Then they complete a survey and then they show up to the appointment and we get on the call with them. And if they buy they become a customer or they don't buy. Right. That is our process from end to end. Well people don't always do what we want them to do through this process. There's a couple of places where they can sort of you know fall out of this funnel. And the first point is when they hit the landing page. And we call this like an exit path or a trap door. So if someone sees our Facebook ad and they click on it and then they come to our landing page. Well they've got to enter the email address to get to the next step and watch the value video. But most people won't enter the email address. You know our KPIs and our benchmarks it's roughly 20% when it's set up properly. So that means out of every 100 people we only get 20 people to enter in their email. And then 80 people don't. So more people exit through this little path here than keep going. So this is one place where people are falling out. And one place where this funnel is leaking. Another place is when they've entered in their email and they're watching the value video. And you know they might watch it. They might watch all of it or they might just watch a little bit of it. Or they might just flick through different points and then they might exit. And you know that person might have been somewhat interested because they did click on our ad. And then they did enter in their email address. And they might have even watched the whole video. But they didn't complete the survey. And then they just quit or something got in the way. And they forgot about it. Right? Well that's another place where they can exit. They can exit without completing that survey there. And these two areas are the main leaks in this funnel. So if our benchmark is a 20% conversion from our landing page to value video. That means that we're losing 80% of our traffic there. And then if our value video benchmark conversion rate is 5%. That means we're losing 95% of our traffic there. Now when you add these up that's a huge leak. And there's nothing wrong with that. Even when we're making those numbers we're still very profitable. And we're still going really well. But we do want to be mindful of these two leaks. And have some sort of thing in place to help. So what do we do about it? What do we do about these two exit paths in our funnel? Well we appear everywhere. And this is what I call achieving omnipresence. And it's how to be everywhere and nowhere at the same time. So it's how to literally be everywhere. It's how you make your niche feel like you're just following them around. Feels like you're just everywhere they look on your shoulder and everything. And the first step to plug these exit paths is to create contingencies. What's a contingency? Well if you remember the two exit paths we had, they were at these two stages. And whenever we have an exit path, we want us to create a contingency. So a contingency is what we do when someone doesn't do what we want them to do. So when a lot of people start not doing what we want them to do, will we create a contingency plan to sort of bring them back? It's like a fail safe for our funnel. So our first one, contingency one, is for people that visited the landing page but didn't opt in. And our second contingency, contingency number two, is for people that visited the value video but didn't complete the survey. And so we want to create two contingency plans here to solve this problem. And we do this with something called retargeting. And you're probably thinking well what's retargeting? Well you might be familiar with retargeting. Well here's a very simple explanation of what retargeting is. So retargeting explained. So with retargeting, you could have a potential customer visiting your website or visiting your funnel or whatever. And then they might opt in or they might view the video or whatever. And then they leave without purchasing or without applying for a strategy session or whatever. Life might get in the way, they might not have enough time. They might be browsing on their phone at their lunch break but then their lunch break's over and they've just got to get back to it. Now a lot of the time when this happens, people are legitimately interested but they just, life gets in the way. And then they forget about it. So it's not like they deliberately left. I mean a lot of the time people want to keep going but they forget. I mean people forget about things all the time. And then later on, one thing's for sure is they're going to use the internet. You know that's the good thing about the internet is that people always use it. So this person who was on your website or who was in your funnel but forgot about it is now going to come back to surf the internet. And then because we've pixeled them, when they visited our website, we dropped a pixel into their browser. And now we can identify them everywhere on the internet and show our ad to them. And now our ad advertises to them specifically and it reminds them of us and the step in the process which they were at. So now this person who was interested but forgot sees our ad everywhere. And then they're either going to click on it or not. And then they come back to the process and many of them go and complete the process by scheduling a strategy session with you or remembering to opt in and watch the value video. And some of them don't anyway and that's totally fine. But retargeting is extremely powerful and it's very simple to set up. And it works really well because we're just retargeting the people who were interested. It's like Facebook ads is great at getting cold people who have never heard of us before interested. But then if they forget, we can use retargeting to kind of get our message back in front of them. It's kind of a way to just keep hitting the people who expressed some interest until they actually take a definitive action. And by using contingencies and retargeting, we're able to appear everywhere in our prospects world. And this is what we call omnipresence, when we just appear everywhere in our prospects mind.
